response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack and web developer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang experience, but the role requires deep hardware-focused expertise: SoC/IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design. Aside from brief C/C++ work on test-and-measurement tooling and some low-latency backend systems, the resume shows no evidence of computer architecture, deep-learning workload optimization, or performance modeling at the silicon level, making this a fundamental domain mismatch for a Principal Performance Architect position.
strengths: Strong systems programming background with DLang, C, and C++, including low-latency, high-throughput services handling millions of requests per second
Meets the formal education-plus-experience bar (BS in Computer Science with well over 8 years of technical experience)
Demonstrated craftsmanship and knowledge-sharing: open-source projects, testing frameworks, workshops, and conference talks
concerns: No SoC or IP modeling, computer architecture, or hardware performance analysis experience anywhere in the work history
No AI/deep-learning workload expertise — no evidence of DNN training/inference optimization, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD work
Career trajectory is centered on web, mobile, and product engineering, far from the principal-level hardware architecture scope of this role

key_factors.factor: Domain mismatch with SoC/AI accelerator architecture
No deep-learning or GPU workload experience
